name: Step 3.7 Flash
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with 1.8B vision encoder + 196B language backbone, 198B total / 11B active
note: Newest StepFun release (successor to Step 3.5 Flash), adding native multimodal (image/GUI/document) support that the text-only Step 3.5 Flash lacked. Not previously in corpus (corpus's newest StepFun entry is Step-3 from 2025-07-31). No shipped 'Step 4' flagship found -- only reported as in training.

results.notes: Note: this is the Artificial Analysis composite Intelligence Index (30), not a GPQA-specific figure -- no single-benchmark independent score could be verified for this exact model. [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing entry mislabeled Artificial Analysis's composite Intelligence Index (30) as a 'gpqa' score -- the record's own verificationNotes already admit this was not a real GPQA figure. StepFun's official GitHub README states a real GPQA Diamond avg@16 item accuracy (over 3168 records) of 78.41% for the NVFP4 checkpoint (no MTP) vs 77.81% for the NVFP4+MTP speculative-decoding checkpoint. No pl]
SWE-Bench Verified score from StepFun's official Step 3.7 Flash launch-blog benchmark table (own column: Step 3.7 Flash 76.5%, vs. e.g. Claude Opus 4.7 87.6%, GPT 5.5 not tested).
Confirmed identically in both the official GitHub README ('secured a definitive second-place finish on SWE-Bench PRO with a score of 56.3') and the official launch blog's benchmark table.

Terminal-Bench 2.1 score from the official launch-blog benchmark table (59.6%); the GitHub README's narrative text and chart tooltip round this to 59.5% -- both cite the same StepFun-run evaluation, minor rounding variance only.

'It reaches 75.82% on BrowseComp, approaching larger models such as Claude Opus 4.7 and GLM 5.1' -- stated directly in StepFun's official launch blog text and confirmed in the accompanying benchmark chart (75.8%).

Text-only (no tool use) Humanity's Last Exam score, from the launch blog's benchmark table row 'HLE w. tool (acc) 47.2% (text-only 49.7%)'. The tool-augmented figure (47.2%) is a different, non-comparable setting and was not used.

Raw GDPval-AA points score (unit matches taxonomy's 'pts, range 0-2000') from the launch blog's benchmark table: 'GDPval-Stirrup 1415.8 (ii 45.8%)'. The normalized percentage form (45.8%) is what StepFun's GitHub README instead reports as 'GDPval-AA: 45.8' -- both numbers describe the same underlying evaluation, but 1415.8 is the correct value for this taxonomy slug's declared point-scale unit.

Not one of the 6 originally-flagged core gaps (mmmu-pro is not a core ranking slug), but surfaced during the mmmu investigation as a genuine, citable, correctly-slugged data point worth capturing rather than discarding.

verificationNotes:
Artificial Analysis independently scored the Intelligence Index at 30 (#34/94); no independently-verified GPQA/LiveCodeBench numbers located for this specific model (only for the prior Step 3.5 Flash), so composite index is the only result reported.

2026-07-16 gap-fill pass on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/livecodebench/mmmu: checked StepFun's own launch materials (GitHub README, HF model card, static.stepfun.com launch blog) and none report MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or MMMU/MMMU-Pro for Step 3.7 Flash -- the launch table was deliberately restricted to a newer agentic/coding suite (ClawEval-1.1, Toolathlon, HLE w. Tool, SWE-Bench PRO, Terminal-Bench 2.1, GDPval-AA, BrowseComp, SimpleVQA, V*). Cross-checked independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis's current Intelligence Index v4.1 methodology (the version used for this model's page) explicitly enumerates its evaluation set as GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, and AA-LCR -- none of the five legacy benchmarks are in AA's current suite at all, and llm-stats.com/organizations/stepfun does not list a Step 3.7 Flash entry, and BenchLM.ai's category breakdown explicitly shows "Math: 0 benchmarks, Not measured" for this model. Sibling model Step 3.5 Flash does carry its own AIME 2025 score elsewhere in third-party trackers, but that figure belongs to a different model and cannot substitute for Step 3.7 Flash's own (unpublished) result. Treated as a legitimate saturated/deprioritized-benchmark absence, not a research gap, for all six core slugs. One partial exception: Artificial Analysis independently measured Step 3.7 Flash on the harder MMMU-Pro variant (75%) -- added under the correct 'mmmu-pro' slug since base MMMU itself remains genuinely unpublished for this model.
